column1: # Verb conjugation — one representative paradigm per class, not a per-verb drill.
# Belongs here: the inflected forms (negative, past, te-form, conditional,
# imperative, volitional, potential, passive, causative) shown on 行く (godan),
# 食べる (ichidan), and the two irregulars する / 来る. Dictionary forms stay in
# the verbs/ decks; type reflects the verb's class.

column4: (する) past negative — did not do
(する) te-form — do and…; please do
(来る) past negative — did not come
(来る) te-form — come and…; please come
(来る) potential/passive — can come; be visited
